zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Sayer於1456年出生在英國艾可可爾斯特可爾斯特 卡素時，他的父親Thomas Sayer26歲，母親Elizabeth Davidson20歲。他於大約1477年與Elizabeth Hawkins在英國英格蘭艾可可爾斯特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1509年2月13日在他的家鄉享年53歲，埋葬在英國艾可可爾斯特聖 比特也斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Sayer于1456年出生在英国艾可可爾斯特可爾斯特 卡素时，他的父亲Thomas Sayer26岁，母亲Elizabeth Davidson20岁。他于大约1477年与Elizabeth Hawkins在英国英格兰艾可可爾斯特结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1509年2月13日在他的家乡享年53岁，埋葬在英国艾可可爾斯特聖 比特也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Sayer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: common occupational name from Middle English sayer, a phonetic variant of Sawyer , which in Wales and neighboring English counties may have been reinforced by the synonymous Welsh saer ‘carpenter’ and everywhere by a possible borrowing into Middle English of Old French seieor, saieur ‘sawyer’.

English: rare occupational name perhaps denoting an assayer of metals, or a servant who tasted his lord's food before serving, from Middle English saiour ‘assayer, tester’ (a shortened form of Old French assaiour, ultimately from Late Latin exagium, a derivative of exagmināre ‘to weigh’).
English: rare occupational name for a professional reciter or minstrel, from Middle English seier(e) ‘speaker’.

variants.name: Sayre
meanings: English (Middlesex and Surrey): variant of   Sayer  .

variants.name: Sears
meanings: Irish (Kerry): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Saoghair ‘son of Saoghar’, a borrowing in Anglo-Norman Ireland of the Anglo-Norman French and Middle English personal name Saher, Seier (Old French Seheri), from ancient Germanic Sigeheri, Sighari, composed of the elements sig‘victory’ + hari, heri ‘army’. See Sayer 5.
English: variant of   Sayers  .
Americanized form of French (or French Canadian) Cyr and French Saint-Cyr (see St. Cyr ), with the addition of excrescent -s, a common feature of Americanized surnames.

variants.name: Sear
meanings: English: variant of   Sayer  .
Americanized form of French (or French Canadian)   Cyr  .

variants.name: Searson
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Saher, Seier (see Sayer 5) + -son.

English: variant of Searston, a habitational name from Syerston (Nottinghamshire). The placename probably derives from the Old English personal name Sighere (genitive Sigheres) + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.
Irish (Tipperary): variant of   Sears  .

variants.name: Sare
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Sayer  .
English: variant of   Sara  .
Croatian (Šare): nickname derived from šar(en) ‘mottled, gaily colored’, possibly applied to someone with hair of different colors. Compare Share .

variants.name: Saier
meanings: South German: occupational name for a farmer, from an agent derivative of Middle High German saien ‘to sow’. Compare Sayer 8.

variants.name: Say
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Sai in Orne or Say in Indre, France, perhaps so called from a Gaulish personal name Saius + the Latin locative suffix -acum (see also French name 3 below).

English: nickname, perhaps for someone who made or sold say, a type of woollen cloth, or for an habitual wearer of clothes made of this material, from Middle English sai(e) ‘say’ (Old French saie, from Latin saga, plural of sagum ‘military cloak’). See also Sayer .

French (southern): topographic name from saix (from Latin saxum) ‘rock’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example, Say in Loire, Saix in Tarn and Vienne, Le Saix in Hautes-Alpes, or Les Saix in Isère.

variants.name: Sayers
meanings: English: variant of Sayer , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. This name is also established in Ireland.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Saoghair; see Sears .

variants.name: Sawyer
meanings: English: occupational name for someone who earned his living by sawing wood, from Middle English sauer(e), sauw(i)er, also sagh(i)er, sag(i)er ‘sawyer’, a derivative of Old English sagu ‘saw’.

Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname, or translation into English of Jewish Seger or some other surname meaning ‘sawyer’, e.g. German Sager and Slovenian Žagar (see Zagar ).

variants.name: Seekings
meanings: English: from the unrecorded Middle English personal name Saykin, a pet form of Middle English and Anglo-Norman French Saher, Seier (see Sayer 5). The final -s is post-medieval and excrescent.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Sayer was born in 1456, in Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, England, his father, Thomas Sayer, was 26 and his mother, Elizabeth Davidson, was 20. He married Elizabeth Hawkins about 1477, in Colchester, Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 13 February 1509, in his hometown, at the age of 53, and was buried in St Peter's Church, Colchester, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Sayer est né en 1456, à Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Sayer, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Davidson, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Hawkins environ 1477, à Colchester, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 13 février 1509, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 53 ans, et a été enterré à St Peter's Church, Colchester, Essex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Sayer 1456 in Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Sayer 26 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Davidson 20. Er hat um 1477 in Colchester, Essex, England, United Kingdom Elizabeth Hawkins geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 13. Februar 1509 im Alter von 53 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in St Peter's Church, Colchester, Essex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Sayer è nato il nel 1456 nel Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, England, il suo padre, Thomas Sayer, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Davidson, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Hawkins circa 1477, nel Colchester, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 13 febbraio 1509, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 53 anni, e fu sepolto a St Peter's Church, Colchester, Essex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Sayerが1456年にイギリスエセックスコルチェスターコルチェスター カストルで生まれた時，父Thomas Sayerは26歳，母Elizabeth Davidsonは20歳であった。約1477年にイギリスイギリスエセックスコルチェスターでElizabeth Hawkinsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1509年2月13日に故郷で53歳で死去し，イギリスエセックスコルチェスタースト ペテル'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Sayer(이)가 1456년 영국 에세스 코체스터 코체스터 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Sayer(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Davidson(은)는 20세였다. 그는 대략 1477년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 코체스터에서 Elizabeth Hawkins(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1509년 2월 13일 그의 고향에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 에세스 코체스터 스트 피터'스 c후르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Sayer nasceu em 1456, em Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Sayer, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Davidson, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Hawkins aproximadamente 1477, em Colchester, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 13 de fevereiro de 1509, em sua cidade natal, com 53 anos, e foi sepultado em St Peter's Church, Colchester, Essex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Sayer родился в 1456 г. в местности колчестир кастл, колчестир, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Sayer было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Davidson, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Hawkins около 1477 г. в местности колчестир, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 13 февраля 1509 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 53 лет и был похоронен в местности ст питир'с чурч, колчестир, эссекс, Англия.
